text.title: django-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mocking in-process code means the test passes regardless of what the service actually does. Only mock at process boundaries.

failure-mode.description: Writing assert result (truthy assertion) instead of assert result.status == 'success'.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Truthy assertions pass against any non-None return value. They provide no coverage of actual behavior.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hardcoded URLs break on URL refactoring. Use reverse() to decouple tests from URL structure.

failure-mode.description: Pulling the user fixture into tests that don't use it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unused fixtures create unnecessary DB rows and slow the suite without benefit.

failure-mode.description: Using baker.make_recipe without registering a recipe first.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Runtime error. Use baker.make() unless a recipe is explicitly defined and registered.

failure-mode.description: Time-dependent assertions like assert order.created_at > yesterday without time-freezing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Flakes around midnight, across CI regions, and in DST transitions. Freeze time with freezegun or monkeypatch.

failure-mode.description: Using sync Client or APIClient for async Django views.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Async views require AsyncClient (Django 4.1+). Sync client misses async-only code branches.

failure-mode.description: Adding tests that import code (raising line coverage) without asserting behavior.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Coverage percentage is signal, not a goal. Tests that don't assert catch no regressions.
